section: Effect of cold rolling and subsequent heat treatment on microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) based austenitic low-density steels

context_update.summary: This chunk covers the abstract, introduction, experimental methods, and initial cold-rolled microstructure results (Section 3.1.1). Three Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) low-density steels are investigated: 28Mn0Ni (Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C), 28Mn5Ni (Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C-5Ni), and 15Mn5Ni (Fe-15Mn-9Al-0.9C-5Ni). Processing includes VIM, homogenization at 1423 K/2.5 h, hot rolling at 1323 K to 13 mm, cold rolling to 50% (HR+CR, ~6 mm), and heat treatment between 873–1273 K/1 h followed by water quenching. Key findings: Ni-containing alloys show delayed recrystallization; after 50% cold reduction, all three alloys exhibit similar hardness, strength, and ductility; heat treatment causes precipitation of intergranular/intragranular B2 and κ-carbides with volume fraction decreasing with temperature; tensile properties are controlled by precipitate fraction and recrystallized austenite; fully recrystallized austenite displays planar slip, dynamic slip band refinement, and dislocation-twin interactions that enhance strain hardening. The 15Mn5Ni alloy cracks beyond 50% cold reduction. In the cold-rolled state, austenite deforms by planar slip without twins/martensite; B2 stringers in 15Mn5Ni thin from ~8 to ~2 μm and shear at 35–45° to rolling direction. Characterization details: EBSD step 120–250 nm, XRD Co-Kα, TEM at 200 kV and −30 °C electropolishing, hardness (30 kg/15 s), tensile (gauge 30 mm, strain rate 3e-4 s⁻¹). Figures: Figure 1 (OIM-EBSD of HR+CR), Table 1 (compositions). Subsequent chunks will present heat-treated microstructures, hardness evolution, tensile properties, and mechanistic discussion.
processing_route: Hot rolling at 1323 K to ~13 mm thickness
Cold rolling to 50% reduction (~6 mm), designated HR+CR
Heat treatment at 873–1273 K for 1 h followed by water quenching
Cracking limit for 15Mn5Ni prevented reduction beyond 50%
key_variables: Volume fraction of B2 and κ-carbide precipitates
microstructure: Planar slip is the dominant deformation mode in cold-rolled austenite
No deformation twins or martensite in any cold-rolled alloy
In 15Mn5Ni, B2 stringers deform concurrently, thinning from ~8 μm to ~2 μm, shearing and fragmenting at 35–45° to RD
Heat treatment produces intergranular and intragranular B2 and κ-carbides; their fraction decreases with increasing temperature
Fully recrystallized austenite exhibits planar slip, slip band refinement, and dislocation-twin interactions
properties: Similar hardness, strength, and ductility for all three alloys after 50% cold reduction
Tensile properties governed by precipitate volume fraction and recrystallized austenite fraction
Enhanced strain hardening and ductility in fully recrystallized austenite
mechanisms: Ni addition delays recrystallization
Planar slip, dynamic slip band refinement, and dislocation-twin interactions enhance strain hardening
B2 stringers accommodate strain and fragment during cold rolling

context_update.summary: The current chunk presents microstructural evolution in cold-rolled (HR+CR) and subsequently heat-treated (973–1173 K) Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) alloys. Cold rolling induces planar slip in austenite with no twins or martensite, and reduces B2 stringers in 15Mn5Ni from ~8 to ~2 µm with shearing at ~35–45° to RD. XRD reveals that after 973 K, both 28Mn0Ni and 15Mn5Ni develop κ-carbides and B2; at 1173 K, 28Mn0Ni is single-phase γ while 15Mn5Ni retains B2. EBSD (Figures 3-5) quantifies recrystallization fraction, grain size, twin fraction, and B2 volume fraction versus temperature and alloy: 28Mn0Ni recrystallizes completely by 1023 K, whereas Ni-containing alloys show delayed recrystallization and higher secondary B2 fraction, especially in 15Mn5Ni. TEM (Figures 6,7) details κ-carbide sizes (10–60 nm in deformed, finer in recrystallized) and two B2 types (intragranular 5–200 nm, intergranular 0.5–1.5 µm). B2 is FeAl-based with Mn and Ni enrichment. These data establish the precipitation-recrystallization interplay preceding property assessment in later chunks.
processing_route: heat treatment at 973 K, 1023 K, 1073 K, 1173 K for 1 h with water quenching

no deformation twins or martensite in cold-rolled state
B2 stringers in 15Mn5Ni thin from ~8 to ~2 µm and shear at 35–45° to RD
deformed and recrystallized austenite after heat treatment
intragranular B2 (5-200 nm) and intergranular B2 (0.5-1.5 µm)
intragranular κ-carbides fine in recrystallized, coarse (10-60 nm) in deformed
B2-rich zone around deformed austenite in 15Mn5Ni
fine B2 along grain boundaries in 28Mn5Ni
prior B2 recovery into bamboo morphology at 1173 K
new B2 precipitates with single-crystal orientation and orientation relationship
mechanisms: B2 stringer fragmentation by dislocation accumulation and substructure development
finer B2 particles dissolve faster than stringers
highly strained regions act as preferential nucleation sites for intragranular B2
Ni addition and lower Mn enhance B2 precipitation and retard recrystallization
B2 undergoes recovery and grain morphology change

context_update.summary: Current chunk presents TEM characterization of heat-treated (973 K and 1173 K) microstructures for the three alloys, revealing κ-carbide and B2 size, distribution, and composition. Recrystallized regions contain fine intragranular κ-carbides while deformed regions show coarser κ-carbides (10–60 nm); B2 particles exist as intergranular (50–200 nm) and intragranular (5–20 nm) types. At 1173 K, 28Mn0Ni is precipitate-free, while Ni-containing alloys show decreasing intragranular B2 with higher recrystallized fraction. TEM-EDS (Table 2) confirms B2 as FeAl-based with significant Mn and Ni, and Ni enrichment increases with temperature. Hardness evolution during cold rolling and subsequent heat treatment is quantified: initial HR hardness values, saturation ~450 HV after 50% reduction, and an increase to 500 HV for Ni-containing alloys at 873 K; hardness drop with temperature is more pronounced for 28Mn0Ni. Tensile stress-strain curves (Fig. 9) are reported, including brittle behavior of 15Mn5Ni at 973–1023 K (<1% elongation), maximum 86% elongation for 28Mn0Ni at 1173 K, and strain hardening exponents ranging from 0.16 to 0.50. Table 3 lists detailed YS/UTS/TE values. These data directly feed into discussions of process-structure-property relationships in subsequent sections.
processing_route: Vacuum induction melting, homogenization 1423 K/2.5 h, hot rolling 1323 K
Heat treatment at 873 K, 973 K, 1023 K, 1173 K (1 h) + water quenching
key_variables: Heat treatment temperature (973 K vs 1173 K)
Mn content (28 vs 15 wt% with Ni)
microstructure: At 973 K: recrystallized grains contain fine uniform intragranular κ-carbides; deformed grains contain inhomogeneous coarse κ-carbides 10–60 nm, coarser in 15Mn5Ni
B2 particles: intergranular in 28Mn0Ni; intragranular (5–20 nm) and intergranular (50–200 nm) in Ni-containing alloys
At 1173 K: no precipitates in 28Mn0Ni; intragranular B2 fraction decreases with increasing recrystallized fraction in Ni-containing steels
B2 is FeAl-based, rich in Mn and Ni; Ni enrichment increases with heat treatment temperature (Table 2)
properties: HR hardness: 28Mn0Ni ~256 HV, 28Mn5Ni ~268 HV, 15Mn5Ni ~375 HV; after 50% cold reduction all ~450 HV
At 873 K: Ni-containing alloys reach 500 HV, 28Mn0Ni unchanged
Hardness drop with temperature more pronounced in 28Mn0Ni
HR+CR tensile: YS 1.35–1.67 GPa, UTS 1.5–1.8 GPa, TE 6–11%, uniform strain <3%
15Mn5Ni brittle after 973 K and 1023 K (TE <1%)
28Mn0Ni 1173 K: maximum TE ~86%
Strain hardening exponent n: 28Mn0Ni 0.16 (973 K) to 0.50 (1173 K); Ni-containing alloys ~0.30 at 1173 K
Two-slope ln(σ)–ln(ε) behavior with n<0.1 at low strain
mechanisms: Highly strained regions act as preferential nucleation sites for intragranular B2 precipitation (1173 K evidence)
Planar slip and dynamic slip band refinement implied as deformation mechanisms in fully recrystallized austenite (previous context)
figures_or_tables: Fig. 6 (TEM 973 K: bright/dark field, κ-carbide and B2)
Fig. 8 (hardness vs cold reduction and heat treatment temperature)
Fig. 9 (engineering stress–strain curves and true stress–strain/ln plots)
Table 3 (tensile properties: YS, UTS, TE, n)

context_update.summary: This chunk completes the tensile response section by reporting strain hardening exponent n values: n for 28Mn0Ni increases from 0.16 at 973 K to 0.50 at 1173 K, while Ni-containing alloys reach only ~0.30 at 1173 K (Fig. 9c,d; Table 3). Fractography (Fig. 10) after 1023 K treatment shows ductile dimple fracture in 28Mn0Ni and 28Mn5Ni, but quasi-cleavage and intergranular cracking along γ/B2 boundaries in 15Mn5Ni. Post-deformation TEM (Fig. 11,12) reveals that in 28Mn0Ni (1173 K) fully recrystallized grains develop homogeneous planar slip and slip band spacing ~200±30 nm with dislocation-twin interactions, while partially recrystallized 973 K sample forms dislocation cells. Ni-containing alloys with coarse κ-carbides and B2 particles show delayed slip band refinement and inhomogeneous slip band spacing; B2 acts as non-shearable obstacles. Discussion begins by rationalizing 50% cold rolling limit to avoid shear bands and notes similar hardness at 50% reduction across alloys. These findings set the stage for further discussion on composition-process-microstructure-property links.
processing_route: Heat treatment at 973–1173 K for 1 h + water quench
TEM of deformed samples at various true strains
key_variables: Homogeneous vs inhomogeneous slip band distribution
microstructure: Dislocation cells in partially recrystallized 973 K 28Mn0Ni
Planar slip and slip band refinement in fully recrystallized 1173 K austenite
Dislocation-κ interaction and twin interaction
B2 particles impede dislocation motion
Intergranular cracks along γ/B2 in 15Mn5Ni
properties: n≈0.30 for Ni-containing alloys at 1173 K
15Mn5Ni <1% elongation at 973 K and 1073 K
Ductile dimple fracture in Mn28 alloys at 1023 K
mechanisms: Taylor hardening from cold work
Slip band refinement and planar slip
Dislocation-B2 interaction and delayed refinement
Intergranular cracking at γ/B2 interfaces

Fractography (Fig. 10) after 1023 K treatment shows ductile dimple fracture in 28Mn0Ni and 28Mn5Ni, but quasi-cleavage and intergranular cracking along γ/B2 boundaries in 15Mn5Ni. Post-deformation TEM (Fig. 11,12) reveals that in 28Mn0Ni (1173 K) fully recrystallized grains develop homogeneous planar slip and slip band spacing ~200±30 nm with dislocation-twin interactions, while partially recrystallized 973 K sample forms dislocation cells. Ni-containing alloys with coarse κ-carbides and B2 particles show delayed slip band refinement and inhomogeneous slip band spacing; B2 acts as non-shearable obstacles. Discussion begins by rationalizing 50% cold rolling limit to avoid shear bands and notes similar hardness at 50% reduction across alloys. These findings set the stage for further discussion on composition-process-microstructure-property links.

context_update.summary: Discussion (Section 4) analyzes how Mn/Ni ratio and heat treatment temperature affect concurrent κ-carbide/B2 precipitation and recrystallization. 4.1 reports that B2 stringers act as PSN sites; intragranular B2 delays recovery. κ-carbides form via spinodal decomposition during quenching. Lowering Mn with Ni coarsens κ-carbides; Ni-rich B2 promotes carbon rejection, aiding κ-carbide formation. In 15Mn5Ni, B2 volume fraction drops from ~40% (973 K) to ~15% (1173 K). 28Mn5Ni shows less recrystallization at 1173 K than 15Mn5Ni. Heat treatment in two-phase region refines grain structure. 4.2 links microstructures to tensile properties: 28Mn0Ni achieves YS~500 MPa, UTS~1000 MPa, TE~86% at 1173 K; at 973 K YS~900 MPa, TE~23%, with processing window around 973 K. 28Mn5Ni with ~10% recrystallization yields 1.3–1.6 GPa strength and 17% ductility; at 1173 K YS=900 MPa, UTS=1250 MPa, TE=39%. 15Mn5Ni has <1% ductility at 973–1023 K, suitable window at 1173 K. Dominant strain hardening mechanisms: planar slip, dynamic slip band refinement, dislocation-twin interactions. Conclusions summarize processing windows and effects of Ni. This chunk completes the discussion and conclusions, covering key process-structure-property links.

28Mn0Ni 973K: YS~900 MPa, TE~23%

Wavy slip and cross slip due to particle inhomogeneity
